options.text: I took over because I wanted to be in charge. I yelled at people if they were late.
I noticed people were burnt out, so I made a fair schedule and gave shout-outs for small wins—sometimes even bought coffee.
I didn't really lead—I just did all the work myself so others wouldn't mess it up.
I waited for someone else to step up because I didn't think I was qualified.

content.explanation: 正确答案体现了领导力的核心：观察需求、公平分配、正向激励和人性化细节（如买咖啡）。干扰项1是专制型领导，不符合团队协作精神；干扰项3是单打独斗，缺乏领导力；干扰项4是逃避责任，与‘step up’完全相反。
content.speakerText: You led a student volunteer group—what made you decide to step up, and how'd you keep everyone motivated?
content.speakerTextCn: 你领导过学生志愿团队，是什么让你决定担起责任？怎么让大家保持动力？

options.text: I told them they were wrong and did it my way anyway.
I stayed quiet because I didn't want to cause conflict.
I said, 'I see it differently—what if we combine your idea with mine?' and we found a better solution.
I complained to the professor and got them in trouble.

content.explanation: 正确答案展示了建设性沟通：尊重他人观点、提出替代方案、寻求共赢。干扰项1是强势对抗，破坏团队信任；干扰项2是回避问题，缺乏主动性；干扰项4是背后告状，不符合职业素养。
content.speakerText: Tell me about a time you disagreed with your team. How'd you handle it?
content.speakerTextCn: 说说你和团队有分歧的时候，你是怎么处理的？
